headline: NDPR +138% YTD: Terbium surges 20.7% in April as heavy rare earth deficit spreads
snippet: Rare earth price momentum accelerated in late April 2026, with NdPr alloy hitting $126/kg (+138% year-to-date) and terbium posting its largest single-month gain since 2023 at +20.7% to $970/kg. Supply deficits have spread from light into heavy rare earth elements, driven by demand for EV motor magnets and defence applications.

headline: COPPER DROPS to $5.92/lb — four straight sessions lower after record $6.12 on April 22
snippet: Copper futures slid to $5.92/lb on April 28, declining for a fourth consecutive session since hitting a record $6.12 on April 22. Stalled US-Iran negotiations threatened sulphur supply chains critical to Chilean copper smelting, while broader manufacturing demand expectations softened under the weight of oil-driven inflation fears.

headline: FIRST BLAST FIRED: Perseus Mining's Yaouré CMA underground — first mechanised mine in Côte d'Ivoire
snippet: Perseus Mining (ASX/TSX: PRU) completed its first underground production blast at the CMA project at its Yaouré Gold Mine in Côte d'Ivoire on April 27, a key milestone for the ramp-up toward steady-state production in Q3 FY27 and the commissioning of the first mechanised underground mine in the country.

headline: WHY GOLD FALLS while the world is on fire: the Iran-oil stagflation paradox explained
snippet: GoldSilver argues that the Iran conflict's near-term effect on gold isn't safe-haven demand — it's oil above $100 feeding inflation expectations, strengthening the dollar, and killing rate-cut hopes. With futures markets now pricing a 52% chance of a 2026 rate hike, precious metals face unusual headwinds even amid active geopolitical risk.

headline: FURY GOLD HITS 12.50 g/t Au over 7.02m at Eau Claire — result sits outside current resource block
snippet: Fury Gold Mines (TSX: FURY) intercepted 12.50 g/t gold over 7.02 metres at Eau Claire in Northern Quebec, approximately 45 metres down-plunge from a prior hole and located outside the existing block model boundary. The result demonstrates grade continuity with resource growth potential from the fully-financed Phase 2 drill program.

headline: NEWMONT'S RECORD QUARTER: $3.1B free cash flow, $6B buyback as gold margins reach $2,800/oz
snippet: Newmont Corporation (NYSE: NEM) reported all-time record quarterly free cash flow of $3.1 billion in Q1 2026, with adjusted EBITDA of $5.2 billion, net income of $3.3 billion, and 1.3 million gold ounces produced. The board authorised an additional $6 billion share repurchase program as S&P Global projects 2026 industry gold margins at a record ~$2,800/oz.

headline: CONTANGO OUTLINES $63M EXPLORATION across Alaska and BC advanced gold project portfolio
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold has outlined a $63 million exploration program for 2026 targeting its consolidated portfolio of advanced gold and critical metals projects across Alaska and Northern British Columbia, one of the larger junior exploration budgets announced this cycle in northern Canada.

headline: COT REPORT APRIL 24: Gold speculative longs near record; silver net length recovers ahead of Fed
snippet: The Commitment of Traders report for gold, silver, and the US dollar index as of April 24 shows speculative gold longs holding near multi-year highs and silver net length recovering from recent consolidation — positioning data as managed money headed into the April 28-29 FOMC meeting that ultimately trimmed the Fed's 2026 rate-cut forecast.

headline: ENERGY STORAGE BOOM lifts lithium demand outlook — AI datacentres add new demand layer in 2026
snippet: Battery energy storage demand for lithium is projected to grow 55% in 2026, with energy storage expected to account for 31% of total lithium consumption — up from 23% in 2025. AI datacenter buildout is adding a new demand layer via grid-scale backup storage, prompting Morgan Stanley to forecast an 80,000-tonne LCE deficit in 2026.

headline: GOLD SWOT: K92 Mining reiterates 2026 production guidance, holds above 50-day moving average
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Gold SWOT highlights K92 Mining (TSX: KNT) reiterating its 2026 production guidance at the Kainantu gold mine in Papua New Guinea while maintaining technical support above the 50-day moving average, reflecting a producer holding steady amid the gold price dip below $4,600.
